opts: to disagree and express opposing views
quiz.explanation: Argue means to exchange opposing views or reasons in a disagreement.

opts: to share information or feelings with others
quiz.explanation: Communicate means to share or exchange information, ideas, or feelings with others.

opts: a large group of people gathered together
quiz.explanation: Crowd means a large number of people gathered together in one place.

opts: to rely on someone or something
quiz.explanation: Depend means to need the support, help, or existence of someone or something.

opts: a container for food or a prepared food
quiz.explanation: Dish means a flat container used for food, or a particular type of prepared food.
Empty means having nothing inside or no people present.
Exact means completely accurate and without any errors or approximations.

opts: newly made or obtained and not stale
quiz.explanation: Fresh means recently produced, picked, or prepared and not decayed or spoiled.

opts: to come together or collect in one place
quiz.explanation: Gather means to come together in a group or to collect things into one place.

opts: to point out or show something
quiz.explanation: Indicate means to point out, show, or suggest something clearly.

opts: a single thing or unit in a list
quiz.explanation: Item means an individual thing or unit, especially one in a list or collection.

opts: to present something for acceptance or refusal
quiz.explanation: Offer means to present something to someone for them to accept or decline.

opts: the amount of money needed to buy something
quiz.explanation: Price means the amount of money required to purchase something.

opts: something made or grown to be sold
quiz.explanation: Product means something that is manufactured or produced for sale.

opts: something owned by a person or land and buildings
quiz.explanation: Property means something that belongs to someone, especially land or buildings.
Purchase means to obtain something by paying money for it.

opts: to suggest something as good or suitable
quiz.explanation: Recommend means to put forward someone or something as being worthy of acceptance.

opts: to carefully choose from a group
quiz.explanation: Select means to carefully pick the best or most suitable option from a group.

opts: a device used to carry out a task
quiz.explanation: Tool means an instrument or device used to carry out a particular function.

opts: to behave toward someone or give a special gift
quiz.explanation: Treat means to behave toward someone in a particular way or to give someone something special.

story.title: A Day at the Market
story.body: Every Friday morning, Nadia and her mother would gather their bags and walk to the town market to purchase fresh items for the week. The market was always full of life — a crowd of buyers and sellers arguing over prices, the smell of fresh food filling the air, and colorful products displayed on every shelf and table. Nadia loved these trips. She treated every visit like an adventure, always eager to explore, select, and discover something new. Her mother, a careful and experienced shopper, had taught her an important rule: never purchase an item without knowing its exact price first. 'Always communicate clearly with the seller,' she would say. 'And if a price is not fair, do not depend on hope — negotiate or walk away.' That morning, Nadia spotted a beautiful dish she wanted to give as a gift. It was displayed on a wooden tool shelf near the entrance. The seller, noticing her interest, came forward to offer it at a high price. Nadia looked at her mother, who indicated with a gentle nod that she should try to bargain. 'Can you recommend a fair price?' Nadia asked politely. The seller smiled and reduced the amount slightly, but Nadia did not argue loudly — she simply gathered her thoughts, remained calm, and responded with a reasonable counter-offer. In the end, both sides agreed. As they left the market, their bags full of fresh vegetables, a selected dish, and several other items, Nadia's mother said, 'You treated that seller with respect, and in return, he treated you fairly. That is how good communication works.' Nadia smiled, appreciating the lesson. She realized that whether you are buying a product, managing property, or simply talking to a neighbor, the tools of kindness, patience, and clear communication are never empty — they always carry great value.

comprehension.q: Where did Nadia and her mother go every Friday?
What rule did Nadia's mother teach her?
opts: never purchase without knowing the exact price

comprehension.q: What item did Nadia want to purchase?
How did Nadia handle the price negotiation?
opts: she stayed calm and made a reasonable counter-offer

comprehension.q: What lesson did Nadia's mother share at the end?
opts: kindness and clear communication carry great value
property is more important than products

listening.title: A Customer and a Shopkeeper
listening.script:
A: Good morning. Can I help you select something today?
B: Yes, I am looking for a fresh dish to serve at a family gathering.
A: We have many products. Let me indicate the best ones for you.
B: Thank you. What would you recommend?
A: This one is very popular. It is made from quality material and the price is fair.
B: What is the exact price?
A: It is five hundred taka. But if you purchase two items, I can offer a small discount.
B: That sounds good. Can you communicate the total price clearly?
A: Of course. Two items will cost nine hundred taka in total.
B: I depend on your honesty as a seller. Is this product fresh?
A: Absolutely. All our products are gathered fresh every morning.
B: Good. I do not want to argue about this but that price is still a little high.
A: I understand. I can treat you as a regular customer and reduce it to eight hundred and fifty taka.
B: That is fair. I will select these two items then.
A: Excellent. Shall I wrap them as a treat for your guests?
B: Yes please. And please indicate on the receipt the exact items I purchased.
A: Of course. Is there anything else you need today?
B: No, this is enough. The shelves looked a little empty when I came in.
A: Yes, we gather new stock every Friday. Come back next week and the crowd will be here too.
B: I will. Thank you for treating me so well.
A: Thank you for your purchase. Please recommend us to your friends.

comprehension.q: What was the customer looking for?
opts: a fresh dish for a family gathering

comprehension.q: What discount did the shopkeeper offer?
opts: a discount if the customer purchases two items

comprehension.q: Why did the customer say the shelves looked empty?
opts: stock was low before Friday restocking

comprehension.q: What did the shopkeeper ask the customer to do at the end?

speaking.prompt: Talk about a time you went shopping. Use words like price, purchase, item, select, offer, fresh, recommend, and treat.
speaking.example: Last weekend I went to the local market to purchase some fresh vegetables and a few other items. The crowd was very large and the noise was exciting. I had to communicate clearly with each seller to get the exact price. One seller tried to argue about the price of a dish I wanted, but I stayed calm and did not depend on him to reduce it — I simply walked to the next stall. Another seller offered me a great deal and recommended a product I had never tried before. I selected it and was very happy. The seller treated me kindly and gave me an extra item as a treat. It was a wonderful experience. I now recommend that market to all my friends.
tips: Start by describing where and when you went shopping
Use price and purchase to talk about what you bought
Describe the crowd and how you communicated with sellers
Use select and recommend to talk about choices
Mention whether the deal was fair and how the seller treated you
End by saying whether you would recommend the place to others

conversation.scenario: A customer visits a shop to buy a gift

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Welcome to our shop. What item are you looking for today?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Say what you are looking for and why you want to purchase it.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: We have many products. Let me indicate a few good options for you.

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Ask the seller to recommend the best one and explain what you need.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: This one is very fresh and popular. What is your exact budget?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Share your budget and ask for the exact price of the item.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: The price is a little high but I can offer a small discount if you select two items.

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Decide whether to accept the offer or argue for a fairer price.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Alright, I will treat you as a special customer. Here is my best price.

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Respond to the offer and say whether you will purchase the item.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Is there anything else you need before you gather your things and leave?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Say whether you need anything else and thank the seller for treating you well.
